titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Frank Trumbull Lee papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15658008.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 6, 2021 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: 746 letters, bank deposit books, checkbooks, cash account books, a stock ledger book, receipts, circulars, brochures, prospectuses, documents, and ephemera by or relating to Colonel Frank Trumbull Lee, 1887-1901. Materials document Lee's business ventures in New Haven, Connecticut; his family life; and his time serving in the Connecticut National Guard. Included is correspondence relating to Lee's mining and real estate ventures in Tallapoosa, Georgia; Kansas City, Missouri; and Kearney, Nebraska.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Brown Rare Books, LLC on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence 1887-1901. II. Papers, 1888-1900.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Colonel Frank Trumbull Lee (1856-1902) of Madison, Connecticut, worked in New Haven as a tobacconist in 1880; was secretary of the New Haven Carriage Company and a member of the firm of Leo & Osborn, machinists; was a real estate dealer; and was an officer of the Imperial Hygenique Fluid Company from 1895 to 1896.
From 1889 to 1893, Lee served with the Connecticut National Guard, New Haven Grays, Company F, Second Regiment.

He was briefly married to Virginia "Bessie" Scranton Lee, with whom he had a daughter, Marion Frances Lee. Following his divorce, Lee lived with his mother, Frances Maria Lewis Lee (1818-1902), and sister, Elizabeth B. Lee (1847-1924), in New Haven.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frank Trumbull Lee Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards.
For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Descriptive information for this collection is drawn largely from the dealer list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 746 letters, bank deposit books, checkbooks, cash account books, a stock ledger book, receipts, circulars, brochures, prospectuses, documents, and ephemera by or relating to Colonel Frank Trumbull Lee, 1887-1901. Materials document Lee's business ventures in New Haven, Connecticut; his family life; and his time serving in the Connecticut National Guard. Included is correspondence relating to Lee's mining and real estate ventures in Tallapoosa, Georgia; Kansas City, Missouri; and Kearney, Nebraska.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Frank Trumbull Lee Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Connecticut. National Guard. Regiment, 2nd. Company F

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged into three subseries: Business, Connecticut National Guard, and Family and Acquaintances. Materials within each file are arranged in order received from dealer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains 746 letters, 27 telegrams, and 16 postcards to or from Colonel Frank Trumbull Lee.

A majority of the letters concern business dealings with bankers and stockbrokers, manufacturers, mining companies, and real estate developers over affairs in New Haven, Connecticut; Tallapoosa, Georgia; Kansas City, Missouri; Kearney, Nebraska; New York, New York; and other locations.
Other letters concern family matters in New Haven, as well as Lee's time serving in the Connecticut National Guard.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by business or business type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 155 letters to Frank Trumbull Lee from bankers and stock brokers, manufacturers, mining companies, and real estate developers.
45 letters relate chiefly to the Imperial Hygenique Fluid Company.
295 miscellaneous letters are from various correspondents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 letters from C. B. Bolmer of Prine & Whitely and 14 from Watson & Gibson to Frank Trumbull Lee concerning the buying and selling of stocks, dividends received, and monies paid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 letters from Chicago Wire Goods Company, 19 from Frank L. Darrow of the Columbus Buggy Company, 3 from L. Dow of the Dow Type Composing Company, 8 from Henry P. Jones of Phineas Jones & Co., 4 from George F. Holcomb of the New Haven Carriage Company, 9 from S. Marsh Young of the Manhattan General Construction Company, and 11 from J. L. Thompson of the Judson Manufacturing Company to Frank Trumbull Lee. Letters concern investments, payments, and other business plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 letters from the Georgia-Alabama Investment Development Company; 3 from G.F. Quackinbush & Co.; 13 from Whitney & Scutt; and 1 from the Tallapoosa Land, Mining and Manufacturing Company to Frank Trumbull Lee. Letters concern the buying and selling of land lots, stocks, and other matters relating to mining land and real property in Tallapoosa, Georgia.

Also present are 12 letters from the Ozark Zinc Development Company; 7 from the Pittsburg and St. Louis Zinc Company; and 2 from the Henson Creek Lead Mines Company to Lee. Letters concern Lee's possible investments and involvement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 letters from C. W. Waddell of the Consolidated Land Company in Kansas City, Missouri; 7 from the Midway Land Company in Kearney, Nebraska; and 10 from the Missouri, Kansas, and Texas Trust Company of Kansas, City Missouri, to Frank Trumbull Lee. Letters concern Lee's investments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 45 letters from Charles E. Granniss, president of the Imperial Hygenique Fluid Company, and other company members to Frank Trumbull Lee. Letters concern the company's pricing, sales, jobbers, costs, investments, and failure.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 295 letters, 27 telegrams, and 16 postcards from various businesses and organizations around the United States to Frank Trumbull Lee. Included are letters from the Republican League of New Haven and the Citizens Law & Order League of New Haven.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 51 letters to Colonel Frank Trumbull Lee from various departments of the Connecticut National Guard. Multiple letters are from Lieutenant-Colonel H. C. Morgan, acting quartermaster general, and document Lee's involvement with the running of the armory and payment of those providing services to the guard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 186 letters to Frank Trumbull Lee from family and acquaintances.

Family members include mother Francis Maria Lewis Lee, wife Virginia "Bessie" Scranton Lee (also known as "Buddie"), brother Reverend William B. Lee; sister Elizabeth B. Lee, and daughter Marion Frances Lee.

Among the correspondents are John B. Doherty of the Connecticut Indemnity Association; George C. Goodon of William Trevor & Co.; James J. Jarman; and Edward E. Bradley of the New Haven Wheel Company. These letters concern both business and personal matters.
Also present are 13 letters from Frank Trumbull Lee to acquaintances and family.

did.unittitle: Bradley, Edward E. to Frank Trumbull Lee
Doherty, John B. to Frank Trumbull Lee
Goodon, George C. to Frank Trumbull Lee
Jarman, James H. to Frank Trumbull Lee
Lee, Elizabeth B. to Frank Trumbull Lee
Lee, Frances Maria Lewis to Frank Trumbull Lee (4 folders)
Lee, Frank Trumbull to various recipients (2 folders)
Lee, Marion Frances to Frank Trumbull Lee
Lee, Virginia "Bessie" Scranton to Frank Trumbull Lee (5 folders)
Lee, William B. to Frank Trumbull Lee (3 folders)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by format. Materials within each file are arranged in order received from dealer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains 46 bank deposit books, checkbooks, and cash account books; a stock ledger book for the Imperial Hygenique Fluid Company; approximately 958 personal and business receipts; 75 circulars, brochures, prospectuses, and other printed material for various business enterprises; 17 pieces of paperwork for various estates, inventories, and liabilities; and 35 pieces of miscellaneous printed and manuscript ephemera.

did.unittitle: Bank deposit books, checkbooks, and cash account books (18 folders)
